items.caption:
Washing machine rubber seal looking a little grubby? Try these two simple cleaning methods! ✨

Bicarbonate of soda paste + a small scrubbing brush to lift dirt and build-up from the seal.

For mould and mildew marks, soak kitchen roll in white vinegar and place it over the affected area for around 15 minutes before removing and running a rinse cycle.

A quick note: vinegar is acidic, but using it occasionally and for a short period like this is generally fine for the rubber seal. I wouldn’t leave it on for hours or use it excessively, and always follow with a rinse cycle afterwards. 🫧

These quick jobs make such a difference and help keep your machine looking and smelling fresh. ✨

Save this for your next washing machine refresh and let me know if you try it! 🤍

#CleaningTips #WashingMachineCleaning #LaundryTips #HomeCleaning #CleaningHack
items.transcript: Got a dirty washing machine rubber seal? I've got you covered. Tip number one, mix bicarbonate of soda with a little water to make a paste. Grab a small scrubbing brush and work it around the seal. Leave it for 15 minutes and then run a rinse cycle. Tip number two, and this one's really good for mould and mildew marks. Soak some kitchen roll in white vinegar and press it onto the marks. Leave for 15 minutes, remove and then run a rinse cycle. And just like that, you're done.

Five Simple Toilet Tuesday Hacks 🚽👇

✨ Bicarb — Great for absorbing odours and lifting grime naturally.

🥤 Coke — The acidity helps break down limescale and stubborn stains.

🦷 Denture tablets — Designed to fizz away bacteria and stains, so they work brilliantly in the loo too.

🫧 Mouthwash — Helps freshen the toilet and leaves it smelling minty clean.

🧴 Washing up liquid — Cuts through grease and grime whilst helping leave the bowl shiny.

Which one have you tried before? 👀

#toilettuesday #toiletcleaning #toilettips #queenofclean
